expectations.text: Names the visual hierarchy problem explicitly
expectations.evidence: 'What you're describing is a lack of visual hierarchy — the page has no clear signal telling the user what's important'

expectations.text: Mentions at least two of the three hierarchy tools: font size, font weight, color/contrast
expectations.evidence: Covers 'Uniform Font Size and Weight' and 'Same Color/Tone for Everything' as two root causes, addressing all three tools

expectations.text: Discusses different levels of content importance (primary, secondary, tertiary)
expectations.evidence: 'Think in Three Levels: Primary (must-see), Secondary (context), Tertiary (background detail)' — explicitly named

expectations.text: Gives specific actionable recommendations with values (e.g., font weights 600-700, grey for secondary text)
expectations.evidence: Gives size ranges ('32–48px', '24–48px') but uses 'bold' rather than specific weight numbers (600/700); colors described as 'muted, lighter gray' and 'very light gray' without hex values. Falls short of the specificity level called for.

eval_feedback.overall: Good set. Assertion 4 is the key discriminator between generic and skill-level specificity.
